name: Slurm Array Retry Plan
summary: Plan safe retries for failed Slurm array tasks.
description: This skill uses read-only Slurm accounting or an exported sacct table to identify failed array tasks, produce a compact retry array range, and generate a reviewable sbatch retry plan before resubmission.

tools.name: sacct
tools.notes: Used to collect completed array-task accounting when available.

tools.name: sbatch
tools.notes: Only used when RUN_SBATCH_RETRY=1 is explicitly set.

inputs.name: array_job_id
inputs.description: Original Slurm array job id to inspect.

inputs.name: sbatch_script
inputs.description: Original or reviewed sbatch script to use for a retry command.

inputs.name: task_manifest
inputs.description: Optional manifest whose rows correspond to array task ids.

inputs.name: retry_concurrency
inputs.description: Optional Slurm array concurrency cap, such as 4 or %4.

outputs.name: failed_task_ids
outputs.description: Sorted unique array task ids whose accounting states match retryable failure states.

outputs.name: failed_array_range
outputs.description: Compressed Slurm array expression for failed tasks, such as 1-3,8,10.

outputs.name: retry_plan
outputs.description: Reviewable Markdown plan with evidence files and an optional sbatch retry command.

outputs.name: failed_manifest_lines
outputs.description: Optional manifest rows corresponding to failed task ids.

examples.title: Mock sacct array accounting for offline testing
Review checklist before retrying array tasks

tests.description: Validate the manifest and referenced examples.
Check shell syntax for the retry planner.
Run against a completed Slurm array job, inspect the failed task range, and submit only after reviewing the generated command.
